title: vauquelinia corymbosa subsp. heterodon
intro: Vauquelinia corymbosa subsp. heterodon, also known as Mexican buckthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Mexican buckthorn are said to include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Mexican buckthorn may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
